description.summary: Allows developers to select multiple AI-generated React components from the Atlas dashboard and download them packaged as a ZIP file.
description.userProblem: Currently, developers must copy-paste generated code one file at a time, which is tedious when trying to bootstrap a new application with 10+ components.
description.proposedSolution: Add a multi-select checkbox UI to the Atlas Grid view and a floating 'Export to ZIP' action bar that triggers a backend compression job.
outOfScope: Exporting directly to a connected Git repository (planned for FEAT-2026-GIT-SYNC)
Transpiling the code to Vue or Angular before export

externalSystems: AWS S3 (for temporary ZIP file storage)
teamDependencies: Cloud Infra team to provision the ephemeral S3 bucket lifecycle rules

uiMockups.interactionNotes: Hovering over a component card reveals a checkbox in the top-left corner. Clicking any checkbox reveals the floating action bar at the bottom of the viewport.
accessibilityRequirements: Checkbox states must be readable by screen readers (aria-checked).
Floating action bar must trap focus when accessed via keyboard.

apiContracts.purpose: Triggers the backend job to fetch the ASTs, compile them into standard .tsx files, and compress them.
apiContracts.dataRequirements: Accepts an array of `component_ids`. Returns a short-lived, pre-signed S3 download URL.
